subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Reference (Step 0 Audit)
- **Standard game** — Setup section states standard play is **2-5 players**.
- **Solo game** — Board section states solo starts at **TR 14** and ends after **Generation 14**.

#### Boards, Cards, and Tiles
- **Game board** — 1 board with TR track, generation track, oxygen track, temperature track, map, standard projects, milestones, and awards.
- **Player boards** — 5 player boards.
- **Corporations** — 17 corporation cards.
- **Projects** — 208 project cards.
- **Reference aids** — 8 reference cards.
- **Ocean supply** — 9 ocean tiles.

#### Markers and Resources
- **Markers/cubes** — 200 player markers/resource cubes.
- **Cube values** — Bronze = 1, Silver = 5, Gold = 10.
- **Global markers** — Temperature marker, oxygen marker, generation marker, first-player marker.

#### Tile Families Used in Play
- **Ocean tiles** — Neutral tiles; placed only in ocean-reserved spaces.
- **Greenery tiles** — Player-owned; raise oxygen if possible.
- **City tiles** — Player-owned; score from adjacent greenery.
- **Special tiles** — Card-defined placement and effects.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Setup Sequence
1. **Place board** — Put the main board in the center.
2. **Load oceans** — Put all 9 ocean tiles on the ocean reserve.
3. **Set oxygen** — Put oxygen marker at 0%.
4. **Set temperature** — Put temperature marker at -30 C.
5. **Set generation** — Put generation marker on 1 (on the TR track area).
6. **Place supplies** — Put cubes and remaining tiles where all players can reach.
7. **Expose board systems** — Keep Standard Projects, Milestones, and Awards fully visible.
8. **Prepare deck** — Use the base project deck for the selected mode and remove mode-excluded cards.

#### Player-Count Header
--- 2-5 Players ---
- **Map/track setup** — Use the exact sequence above.

- **Map/track setup** — Use the same board setup sequence above.
- **Solo timer track rule** — Solo game ends after Generation 14.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Shared Setup Tasks
- **Color choice** — Each player takes one color's markers and one player board.
- **Production tracks** — Put production markers on starting spaces from setup/corporation effects.
- **Resources** — Place starting cubes in matching resource boxes.
- **Corporation choice** — Choose corporation from dealt options.
- **Opening projects** — Buy kept opening project cards for 3 M€ each.

#### Player-Count Header
--- 2-5 Players ---
- **Starting TR** — Each player starts at TR 20.
- **Turn order scope** — All players take part in every generation phase.

- **Starting TR** — Solo starts at TR 14.
- **Generation cap** — Solo ends after Generation 14.

#### Corporation Setup Notes
- **Starting money/resources** — Take exactly what your chosen corporation grants.
- **Starting production** — Apply corporation production adjustments immediately.
- **Fixed opening effects** — Resolve any corporation text that instructs setup actions.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Start Procedure
1. **Determine first player** — Randomly choose first player and give the first-player marker.
2. **Finalize corp setup** — Apply each corporation's starting resources/production/effects.
3. **Validate global tracks** — Confirm oxygen 0%, temperature -30 C, oceans 9 in reserve, generation 1.
4. **Begin play** — Start Generation 1 with Turn Order phase, then Research phase.

#### Player-Count Header
--- 2-5 Players ---
- **First player flow** — First-player marker rotates clockwise each generation.

- **First player flow** — Single player takes all phases each generation.
- **End trigger override** — Game still uses global-parameter completion, but solo also has the Generation 14 limit from rulebook.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Generation Order
1. **Turn Order Phase** — Confirm player order and rotate first-player marker clockwise.
2. **Research Phase** — Each player draws 4 project cards and may buy any for 3 M€ each.
3. **Action Phase** — In order, players take 1-2 actions per turn until all players pass.
4. **Production Phase** — Produce resources, convert leftover energy to heat, gain M€ = TR + M€ production, then advance generation.

#### Player-Count Header
--- 2-5 Players ---
- **Round loop** — All players cycle through Action turns until everyone passes.

- **Round loop** — Single player executes phase sequence each generation.
- **Solo end clock** — Stop no later than end of Generation 14.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Core Actions (Action Phase)
- **Play a project card** — Pay card cost and meet all requirements before resolving effects.
- **Use a blue-card action** — Use once per generation per card; mark usage with a marker.
- **Use a Standard Project** — Pay listed M€ and resolve the printed project effect.
- **Convert plants** — Spend 8 plants to place 1 greenery tile.
- **Convert heat** — Spend 8 heat to raise temperature 1 step.
- **Milestones/Awards** — Claim/fund according to board rules and costs.

#### Tile Placement Rules
- **Ocean placement** — Place only in blue ocean-reserved spaces.
- **Ocean reward** — Placing an ocean raises TR by 1.
- **Greenery adjacency** — If possible, place greenery next to your own tile(s).
- **Greenery terraform** — Greenery raises oxygen and TR only if oxygen can still increase.
- **City adjacency limit** — City tiles may not be placed next to another city, except card-specific exceptions.
- **Area bonuses** — Gain printed placement bonus on the chosen area.
- **Ocean adjacency bonus** — Gain 2 M€ for each adjacent ocean tile when placing any tile.

#### Resource Spending Constraints
- **Steel use** — Steel pays only for building-tag cards; value 2 M€ per cube.
- **Titanium use** — Titanium pays only for space-tag cards; value 3 M€ per cube.
- **Overpay rule** — No refund when steel/titanium overpay card costs.
- **M€ production floor** — M€ production can be negative but never below -5.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Global Parameters
- **Three shared parameters** — Temperature, oxygen, and oceans are the terraforming parameters.
- **TR gain rule** — Whenever you raise one parameter, you also raise TR by the same amount.
- **Maxed parameter rule** — If a parameter is already at target, ignore that increase and gain no TR from it.

#### Bonus Steps and Thresholds
- **Track bonuses** — Some parameter steps grant extra bonuses immediately when reached.
- **Oxygen threshold** — Oxygen track includes a bonus step at 8%.
- **Temperature thresholds** — Temperature track includes heat-production and ocean-related bonus steps.

#### Tile Ownership and Scoring Geometry
- **Ocean ownership** — Oceans are neutral and owned by no player.
- **Greenery value** — Each greenery is worth 1 VP at endgame.
- **City value** — Each city scores 1 VP per adjacent greenery, regardless of greenery owner.

#### Card-Type Timing
- **Green cards** — Resolve immediate effects when played.
- **Blue cards** — Provide ongoing effect and/or repeatable action (action once per generation).
- **Red event cards** — Resolve one-time event effects.

#### Requirement Timing
- **Play-time check** — Requirements must be met when the card is played.
- **Post-play persistence** — Requirement does not need to remain true after card enters play unless text says otherwise.

#### Solo Variant Rules
--- 1 Player ---
- **Starting TR** — Start at TR 14 instead of 20.
- **No milestones or awards** — Milestones and Awards are not used in solo play.
- **Neutral opponent** — A virtual opponent exists for resource/production removal effects.
- **Neutral cities** — Place 2 neutral city tiles on the map with adjacent greenery before choosing cards.
- **Generation cap** — Must complete terraforming (all 3 global parameters at goal) before end of Generation 14.
- **Loss condition** — If not terraformed by end of Generation 14, you lose.
- **Post-game greenery** — Convert remaining plants to greenery without raising oxygen, then score VP.
- **Tharsis Republic exception** — If you choose to play Tharsis Republic in solo mode, you gain M€ production for the **2** neutral cities even though they are placed before you reveal your corporation.

#### Corporate Era Variant
- **No extra starting production** — In Corporate Era, players start with no extra production of resources (the standard game grants **1** of each production type at setup). This affects all player counts.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **Terraforming completion** — End is triggered when oxygen reaches 14%, temperature reaches +8 C, and all 9 oceans are placed.
- **Generation completion** — Finish the current generation through Production phase before scoring.

#### Player-Count Header
--- 2-5 Players ---
- **Standard end** — Use global-parameter completion trigger above.

- **Solo cap** — End no later than after Generation 14 per rulebook note.

#### VP Sources (All Required)
- **TR points** — Score VP equal to final Terraform Rating.
- **Milestones** — Score VP from claimed milestones.
- **Awards** — Score VP from funded-award rankings.
- **Board tiles** — Greenery: 1 VP each; City: 1 VP per adjacent greenery (any owner).
- **Played cards** — Score printed VP symbols and card-text endgame VP.

#### Tiebreakers
- **First tiebreak** — Highest M€ total wins tied VP.
- **Second tiebreak** — If still tied, shared victory.

#### 2-Player Award Exception
--- 2 Players ---
- **No second place** — In a 2-player game, second place in funded awards gives 0 VP instead of 2 VP.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### First 2-3 Generations
- **Card discipline** — Keep only project cards you can realistically play soon after paying 3 M€ buy cost.
- **Income first** — Prioritize early production growth over low-efficiency early VP cards.
- **Tag matching** — Favor cards your starting resources can actually fund (steel for building, titanium for space).

#### Board Positioning
- **First tile placement** — Take placement bonuses that fund your next action, not just immediate points.
- **City-greenery lane** — Reserve spaces where your future greenery can feed your own city scoring.
- **Ocean leverage** — Place near oceans when possible to capture repeated 2 M€ adjacency boosts.

#### Tempo Control
- **Parameter timing** — Claim TR-gaining parameter raises early when efficient.
- **Milestone plan** — Identify one milestone path early and commit before opponents lock it.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Frequent New-Player Errors
- **Overbuying research** — Keeping too many cards drains cash and delays your engine.
- **Dead-card hoarding** — Holding requirement-locked expensive cards too long stalls action quality.
- **Illegal resource use** — Spending steel/titanium on wrong tag types is not allowed.
- **Overpay waste** — Paying with excess steel/titanium gives no change and silently loses value.
- **Bad city timing** — Dropping cities without adjacent greenery plan creates weak endgame conversion.
- **Award/milestone delay** — Waiting too long forfeits high-value shared objectives to opponents.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision Set A: Terraform vs Engine
- **TR now** — Parameter raises give immediate income + VP through TR.
- **Engine later** — Production/tableau scaling can outscore late if built early enough.

#### Decision Set B: Board vs Cards
- **Map commitment** — Cities/greenery convert strongly if geometry is planned.
- **Tableau commitment** — Card synergies can win without heavy board focus if economy is tight.

#### Decision Set C: Shared Objective Timing
- **Milestone race** — Claim as soon as you secure eligibility and cash.
- **Award race** — Fund when you can realistically hold a payout rank by game end.

metadata.notes: Reformatted for scan-first readability. Step 0 player-count audit integrated into content headers. Primary source remains the provided Terraforming Mars rulebook text.
